definition: Dispelling or driving out an arisen unwholesome pattern, especially in the MN 2 outflow sequence.
notes: In MN 2 this follows the arising of thoughts tied to sensuality, ill will, or harmfulness. Keep it as active clearing-away language. Do not let it disappear back into generic abandoning, because the sutta already uses the broader pahāna family for the whole sequence.

example_phrases.translation: outflows are to be abandoned by dispelling
example_phrases.notes: One member of the sevenfold MN 2 method sequence.

example_phrases.translation: one dispels an arisen sensual thought
example_phrases.notes: Shows the active clearing-away force behind the noun.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'dispelling' as the default rendering in the MN 2 method-sequence context and nearby practice prose.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Use 'driving out' or 'getting rid of' only when the local prose clearly benefits. Do not flatten the term into unmarked abandoning-language or recast it as suppression.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Keeps vinodanā distinct from generic pahāna language within the sevenfold outflow sequence.
